description_long: This is a medium-sized barn with a prominent gambrel roof, featuring a wide, gently sloping upper section and a steeper lower section. The exterior is clad in a brick-patterned siding, giving it a robust and traditional appearance. The front facade is distinguished by a large, double-hinged barn door with a crossbuck design, flanked by two rectangular flower boxes filled with colorful flowers. Above the main entrance, there is a loft door with a matching crossbuck pattern, suggesting a hayloft or storage area. Small, horizontally oriented windows are present on both sides of the barn, providing natural light to the interior. The overall design is reminiscent of a classic American barn, suitable for agricultural or decorative use in an urban or suburban setting.
description: A classic barn with a gambrel roof, brick-patterned siding, double crossbuck doors, and flower boxes flanking the entrance. Small horizontal windows and a loft door complete its traditional American barn look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the double crossbuck doors open, flower boxes on either side, and a loft door above the entrance.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the wide, gambrel roof with its distinct two-slope profile and no visible doors or windows.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the closed double crossbuck doors, flower boxes, and small horizontal windows, mirroring the front but with doors shut.
description_view_3: Top-down view from the rear, again emphasizing the gambrel roof's shape and the absence of visible entry points.
attribute_door_style: double hinged doors with crossbuck design, one set at the main entrance and one loft door above
attribute_window_arrangement: two small horizontal windows on each side of the main doors, symmetrically placed
